payload.text: Permitir que seja arrastado um projeto diretamente para dentro do site, adicione esse possibilidade. Mexa somente nisso, não modifique nem altere mais nenhuma outra função.

**Focusing on Drag and Drop**

I'm zeroing in on integrating direct "drag and drop" capability for project files (JSON) into the application, as requested. The main task is to implement this specific feature. I'm not to be concerned with any related aspects, the core implementation of "drag and drop" functionality is my current focus.


**Implementing Drag and Drop**

I've added `onDragOver` and `onDrop` handlers to `App.tsx`'s main container. My `onDrop` handler now prevents default behavior, extracts the file, and attempts to parse it as JSON. I'm validating the JSON against `carouselSchema`, just like in `handleImportProject`, and updating the application state if the file passes. I'm carefully making sure to make minimal changes, as requested. I'm planning to provide visual feedback during the drag-over, for better user experience.


**Refining Drop Handler**

I'm now implementing a global drop handler on the root container. My `onDrop` logic is in place now, including preventing default behavior and stopping propagation, as well as handling the file type and parsing the JSON content. I'm validating the JSON against `carouselSchema` and updating the application state accordingly, the same as the initial approach. I'm focusing on simplicity, since the main goal is to be able to drag a project into the site, as directly requested.


**Integrating File Handling**

I'm now integrating the file handling logic. I'm focusing on incorporating the JSON parsing, schema validation, and state updates. My plan is to reuse or adapt the `handleImportProject` logic within a new `processProjectFile` helper, to keep it self-contained. I will add `onDragOver` and `onDrop` events on the main `div` in `App.tsx`.


**Finalizing Event Handlers**

I'm now implementing the `handleGlobalDrop` and `handleDragOver` functions directly within the `App` component. I'm attaching these handlers to the root `div`, in order to comply with the minimal-change and scope requirements. I will focus on only this aspect as directed, without any extraneous features. I am checking if visual states are necessary.


**Refactoring Import Logic**

I'm now refactoring the core logic for handling dropped project files. I'm taking the existing code, from the drop handler, and simplifying the flow. I'm focusing on parsing the JSON file, validating it against `carouselSchema`, and updating the application state. I'm incorporating the file reader functionality to read the contents of the dropped file. I'm reusing the existing `showToast` and state update functions to maintain consistency. My focus is on making the import process robust and error-proof, given that the application size is large. I've also incorporated the `handleDragOver` function to prevent default browser behavior and ensure the drop event is correctly captured.


**Adding Visual Feedback**

I'm now integrating visual feedback during the drag-over operation, which is very important. I'm adding `isDraggingFile` state to manage the visual indication and adding the `handleDragOver` to set `isDraggingFile` to `true`. I will also add `onDragLeave` to set it to `false`. Then I will add an overlay that appears when the `isDraggingFile` is `true`, with a visual prompt for the user.


**Optimizing User Experience**

I'm now refining the visual feedback for the drag-and-drop feature. I've switched from using the continuous `onDragOver` event to the `onDragEnter` and `onDragLeave` events. I'm leveraging a full-screen, transparent overlay to provide clear visual cues to the user when a file is being dragged over the application window. This implementation ensures a more accurate and intuitive user experience, and does not violate the scope or function requirements. I will check for any necessary edge cases, especially since `onDragLeave` triggers on nested elements.


**Implementing File Loading**

I'm now incorporating a `loadProjectFile` function to encapsulate the file reading and JSON parsing, so that I can reuse code. I'm focusing on the `App.tsx` file for adding the state and event handlers. I'll add `isDraggingFile` state using `useState`. I can leverage the existing `handleImportProject` logic by moving common code to a separate `loadProjectFile` helper. My goal is to keep things simple and avoid extra complexity, as required.


**Refactoring Loading Process**

I'm now refactoring the `loadProjectFile` to improve error handling and simplify logic. I'm focusing on consolidating all file-related functionality within the `App` component. I'm utilizing `useState` to manage `isDraggingFile`, which drives the visual feedback. I'm leveraging the existing import logic to ensure consistency and robustness, and ensuring that any errors are clearly reported to the user. I'm continuing to prioritize adherence to the initial requirements, as requested.


**Adding Drop Logic**

I'm now implementing the drag and drop functionality strictly as specified. I'm adding `isDraggingFile` state to `App.tsx` and incorporating `handleDragOver`, `handleDragLeave`, and `handleDrop` functions. I will wrap the main content with the appropriate event handlers, and add the visual drag overlay, all without altering any other functions.


**Ensuring Function Accessibility**

I'm now making sure that the logic encapsulated in `loadProjectFile` can access all of the required state setters within `App.tsx`. Specifically, I'm focusing on ensuring access to `setIsDraggingFile`, `setDslInput`, `setCurrentProjectId`, `setSaveFormData`, `setCurrentIndex`, `setView`, and `showToast`. I'm verifying the scoping is appropriate.

entries.description: Adicionada funcionalidade de arrastar e soltar (drag and drop) para importar arquivos de projeto JSON diretamente no aplicativo, com feedback visual aprimorado.

payload.text:
Consegue criar na aba JSON, uma outra aba lá, de edição de texto? onde eu consiga mudar, excluir os textos, mudar a cor, destacar, tirar destaque, sem ter que mexer diretamente no código? isso apenas mexendo nesse pedaço e aí a plataforma sozinha muda no código, coloque tudo que for possível e relevante, tamanho da fonte, espaçamento, destaque e etc
Não mude mais nada. Somente isso

**Adding Text Editing Tab**

I'm focusing on incorporating a new "Text Editing" tab. This will sit alongside existing options like "JSON," providing a space for code-driven visualization. My immediate goal is to establish the tab's integration within the overall UI flow.


**Implementing Block Controls**

I'm now integrating the block controls within the new tab. The goal is to allow users to edit text content and format elements. I am updating the main application state to include a new `'BLOCKS'` tab value, adding a sidebar section to manage the blocks. This section will render a list of the blocks in the current slide, with input fields for modification.


**Designing Block Controls**

I'm deep in the design of the block controls now. Each block will be a card with a textarea for content, and buttons for delete, highlight toggle, color picker, and range sliders for font size, line height, and spacing. I need to make the alignment selection intuitive. Updating blocks in the DSL looks feasible with deep cloning, and the existing update functions look like they will handle it well. I'm importing icons for sorting and deletion. The new tab order is finalized.

Para atender ao seu pedido de criar um editor de blocos visual e intuitivo, vou adicionar uma nova aba chamada **"CONTEÚDO"** (entre ASSETS e JSON). 

Esta aba permitirá gerenciar todos os blocos do slide atual: editar textos, adicionar/remover itens de lista, ajustar tipografia (tamanho, peso, cor), gerenciar destaques e excluir blocos, tudo refletindo em tempo real no código DSL sem que você precise tocar no JSON.

### Especificação Técnica:
1.  **Nova Aba "CONTEÚDO"**: Ícone de `Type` na sidebar.
2.  **Lista Dinâmica de Blocos**: Mapeia todos os blocos do slide selecionado.
3.  **Editor de Texto Inteligente**: 
    *   Área de texto para conteúdo.
    *   Botões rápidos para inserir `[[Destaque]]` (fundo colorido) e `**Negrito**`.
4.  **Controles de Design por Bloco**:
    *   Sliders de Tamanho da Fonte, Espaçamento de Letras e Altura de Linha.
    *   Seletor de cor individual por bloco.
    *   Troca de variante (ex: mudar um CARD de 'default' para 'accent').
5.  **Gerenciamento de Estrutura**: Botão para excluir o bloco e (no caso de listas) adicionar ou remover linhas.

entries.description: Adicionada a aba 'BLOCKS' para edição visual de conteúdo, estilos tipográficos e gerenciamento de blocos sem necessidade de editar o JSON diretamente.

payload.text:
Troque a logo do app pela nossa:
https://ik.imagekit.io/zslvvoal4/Logo%20Impact.webp?updatedAt=1761153002773

E a cor principal, substitua esse verde pela nossa: #1fb2f7

**Initiating Logo Update** I'm now focused on the logo update. My initial assessment is straightforward: Swap the existing logo with the new URL `https://ik.imagekit.io/zslvvoal4/Logo%20Impact.webp?updatedAt=17611530027`. This seems simple, a direct substitution. I'm considering the potential impact on UI layout and image loading speed to ensure a smooth transition. **Analyzing Color Branding** I'm now diving into the color branding update. I'm focusing on replacing the emerald green with the brand's blue. I've identified the use of the emerald green across Tailwind classes in `App.tsx`. I'll replace those instances and use a custom color definition to ensure the exact blue is reflected. Given that I have access to edit `index.html`, I'll introduce the color definition there, for a minimal update. **Refining Color Implementation** I'm now revising my color strategy. It seems the best path forward is to introduce a Tailwind custom color mapping, which seems better than trying to rename classes, since I can map the new hex directly to the emerald usages. I'll define `primary` in `index.html` as `#1fb2f7` to ensure that all `emerald` instances are updated to the `primary` defined in tailwind. Next I'll replace the logo in the `App.tsx` file for all the view, and UI instances. **Implementing Color Replacement** I'm now implementing the color replacement strategy. I'll add the Tailwind configuration snippet to `index.html` to define the custom `brand` color as `#1fb2f7`. This will allow me to replace instances of `emerald` with `brand` in `App.tsx` and other related files, such as `SlideCanvas.tsx`, and `BlockRenderer.tsx`. I will also replace the logo with the provided image URL in `App.tsx`. I will check other files as needed, to catch all instances. **Finalizing Color Integration** I'm now putting the finishing touches on the color integration, and logo replacement. In `App.tsx`, I'm methodically replacing all instances of `emerald` with `brand`, and introducing the image URL into the Dashboard and Editor headers. I'm focusing on those specific areas to align with the update's scope. I've confirmed no need to update the `TOKENS`. I'm verifying the changes, and I am almost done. I'm checking the `SlideCanvas.tsx` file too. **Executing UI Adjustments** I'm now implementing the final UI adjustments. I've updated `index.html` to include a custom color definition for `brand` as `#1fb2f7`. I am replacing the green logo box in `App.tsx`, for the Dashboard header and Editor sidebar, with an `` tag using the provided URL, and I'm updating the Drag & Drop upload with the new `brand` blue. I'm methodically replacing all `emerald` instances with `brand` in `App.tsx` and now I'm checking `SlideCanvas.tsx` for any remaining emerald-related UI components to update them as well.

entries.description: Adicionada configuração do Tailwind para definir a cor da marca (#1fb2f7) e permitir o uso de classes personalizadas.
Substituída a logo pelo URL fornecido e atualizada a cor principal de emerald para brand (#1fb2f7) em toda a interface.
Atualizadas as referências de cor emerald para a cor da marca (brand) e removidos possíveis conflitos de estilo.
Atualizada a cor de feedback do ícone editável de emerald para a cor brand (#1fb2f7).
Atualizada a cor de feedback do ícone editável de emerald para a cor brand (#1fb2f7).

payload.text: Pode tirar a escrita Impact Render e deixar só a logo maior

**Refining The App's Visuals**

I've been focusing on the app's visual identity. My immediate concern is the logo, specifically removing "Impact Render" from it. Following that, I'm working to increase the logo's overall size to improve visibility. Currently, the app uses a logo from this URL: `https://ik.imagekit.io/zslvvoal4/Logo%20Impact.webp?updatedAt=1761`.

entries.description: Removida a escrita 'Impact Render' do cabeçalho do Dashboard e da Sidebar do Editor, deixando apenas a logo com um tamanho maior para destaque.

payload.text: Pode ser um pouco menor, ficou muito grande a logo

**Assessing Logo Dimensions**

I'm currently focused on the logo's dimensions within the application. My initial observation is that the current height, set to "h-16" in the `App.tsx` file, appears to be the culprit behind the perceived excessive size. I'm exploring potential adjustments to this height value to achieve a more appropriate visual balance.

entries.description: Reduzido o tamanho da logo no Dashboard (de h-16 para h-10) e na Sidebar do Editor (de h-10 para h-8) para um visual mais equilibrado.
